description:
Second sliver of VR.3: typed INT entry-frame slots end-to-end. Mirrors VR.3.1's float pattern.

## Why this is atomic

Same chicken-and-egg as .5.1: schema-kind and storage-format must flip together to keep GC parity. Int slots store raw i64 (no tag bits, no shift) and SSA values flow raw through arithmetic, with boundary box-up via `box_int` (shl 3 | 1).

## Adds

- Per-fn schema build: `descr_is_int(entry_param) → FieldKind::RawBytes(8)`.
- Entry-frame param load for int slots: `load.i64` raw (storage already raw, no shift needed).
- Per-fn `raw_int_vars: HashSet<Var>` mirroring .5.1's `raw_float_vars`.
- `as_tagged` helper (introduced in .5.1) extended: if var is in `raw_int_vars`, emit `box_int` (shl 3 | 1).
- lower_prim int fast paths (BinOp::{Add,Sub,Mul,Div,Mod,Lt,Le,Gt,Ge,Eq,Neq} with both operands `descr_is_int`):
  - Skip `unbox_int` when operand is in `raw_int_vars`.
  - Result raw → insert into `raw_int_vars`, skip `box_int`.

## Kills

- `unbox_int(av)` per-op call when av is already raw.
- `box_int(raw)` per-op call when the result Var will be consumed by another typed-int op.
- The `(n << 3) | TAG_INT` boundary materialization for int entry params on every read — collapsed to one shl at the boundary.

## Acceptance

- Extend fixture `vr1_int_arith.fz` (or new sibling `vr3_int_args.fz`) with a fn `fn sum3(a, b, c) do a + b + c end` (or annotated equivalent if ir_typer needs help). CLIF asserts no `sshr_imm` or `ishl_imm 3` between back-to-back arith ops on a/b/c.
- Matrix green.

## Depends on

- fz-ul4.27.5.1 (introduces the `as_tagged` helper + `raw_*_vars` env infrastructure).

## UNBLOCKS

- fz-ul4.27.5.3 (continuations need both raw kinds working in entry frames first)
title: VR.3.3: typed INT entry-frame slots end-to-end
